heading: Penalties
label: 1705 Penalties
notes: Amendments2024—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 118–502007— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text of section read as follows:Pub. L. 110–96“(a) A civil penalty of not to exceed $50,000 may be imposed on any person who violates, or attempts to violate, any license, order, or regulation issued under this chapter.“(b) Whoever willfully violates, or willfully attempts to violate, any license, order, or regulation issued under this chapter shall, upon conviction, be fined not more than $50,000, or, if a natural person, may be imprisoned for not more than twenty years, or both; and any officer, director, or agent of any corporation who knowingly participates in such violation may be punished by a like fine, imprisonment, or both.”2006—Subsec. (a). , substituted “$50,000” for “$10,000”.Pub. L. 109–177, § 402(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “twenty years” for “ten years”.Pub. L. 109–177, § 402(2)1996—Subsec. (a). , inserted “, or attempts to violate,” after “violates”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 1422(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “, or willfully attempts to violate,” after “violates”.Pub. L. 104–201, § 1422(2)1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “$10,000” for “$50,000”.Pub. L. 102–396substituted “$50,000” for “$10,000”.Pub. L. 102–393

children.heading: Unlawful acts
children.label: a Unlawful acts
children.text: It shall be unlawful for a person to violate, attempt to violate, conspire to violate, or cause a violation of any license, order, regulation, or prohibition issued under this chapter.
an amount that is twice the amount of the transaction that is the basis of the violation with respect to which the penalty is imposed.

children.heading: Criminal penalty
children.label: c Criminal penalty
children.text: A person who willfully commits, willfully attempts to commit, or willfully conspires to commit, or aids or abets in the commission of, an unlawful act described in subsection (a) shall, upon conviction, be fined not more than $1,000,000, or if a natural person, may be imprisoned for not more than 20 years, or both.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An action, suit, or proceeding for the enforcement of any civil fine, penalty, or forfeiture, pecuniary or otherwise, under this section shall not be entertained unless commenced within 10 years after the latest date of the violation upon which the civil fine, penalty, or forfeiture is based.

children.heading: Commencement
children.label: B Commencement
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the commencement of an action, suit, or proceeding includes the issuance of a pre-penalty notice or finding of violation.

children.heading: Time for indictment
children.label: 2 Time for indictment
children.text: No person shall be prosecuted, tried, or punished for any offense under subsection (c) unless the indictment is found or the information is instituted within 10 years after the latest date of the violation upon which the indictment or information is based.
